title: Reginald Barkbone
content:

{{Quote|…Barkbone lost his leg plundering the palaces of Hapes […] and lost his mind sailing through the fire rings of Fornax […] Barkbone substituted the absent leg with a peg of cybernetic design, though I sadly cannot say the same for his brain.|Grand Admiral Rufaan Tigellinus|<ref name="app" />}}
'''Reginald Barkbone''', Scourge of the Seven Sectors, was a swashbuckling [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] pursued by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A [[Poss'Nomin]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], he was the figure of much myth, infamous for many audacious heists and daring escapades in his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Robber Baron]]''. Barkbone was noted for his flamboyance: he sported a [[Lightfoil/Legends|lightfoil]] and spoke [[Lok Pirate's Cant]] and also kept a [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]] atop his shoulder to repeat his every word.

Barkbone's most famous exploits included a raid on the palaces of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]], a fracas with [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Guards]] on [[Axion]], and a successful voyage through the [[Fire Rings of Fornax]]—at the cost of his [[Limb/Legends|leg]], [[Eye/Legends|eye]], and sanity, respectively. The [[Loronar navy]] attempted to apprehend Barkbone, vainly pursuing the Poss'Nomin bandit for a [[Standard month/Legends|month]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] made Barkbone's apprehension a priority, tasking [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] with his capture. Tigellinus chased the relentless freebooter around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to no avail, but was determined to neutralize the threat Barkbone posed.

==Biography==
===Scourge of the Seven Sectors===
{{Quote|Me worst spell in space? No, 'twarnt sailing through the fire rings of Fornax. 'Twas the month I spent on the run from the Loronar navy, an' stuck with a vegetarian galley droid.|Reginald Barkbone|<ref name="negtd">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Planet21-Fornax-SWR.png|thumb|right|185px|The Fire Rings of Fornax]]
Reginald Barkbone, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the three-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[Poss'Nomin]] [[Species/Legends|species]], lived as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. A being surrounded by myth and supposition,<ref name="app" /> he had become infamous by the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], earning the moniker, "Scourge of the Seven Sectors."<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] was unaware as to what "[[Seven Sectors]]" Barkbone's appellation referred. At various points in his career, Barkbone was active in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]], the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]], and the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]], striking at an array of targets. His [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Robber Baron]]'', was a stolen [[Star Galleon-class frigate|''Star Galleon''-class frigate]], though he also had a number of [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] [[Corsair/Legends|corsairs]] at his disposal. He was known as a sly, savage operator, mentioned in the same breath as [[Gir Kybo Ren-Cha]], the [[Thalassian slavers/Legends|Thalassian slavers]], and the [[Black Hole Gang/Legends|Black Hole Gang]], all among the most notorious marauders operating in the Outer Rim.<ref name="app" /> On many occasions, Barkbone had run-ins with the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and he frequently vexed the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Rovan Tresario]], which later led Tresario to found [[House Tresario]], a chapter of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] that specialized at hunting pirates. However, all efforts by the chapter to capture Barkbone proved unsuccessful.<ref name="tbhc">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>

Barkbone was infamous for a slew of implausible, heroic feats. His most famous accomplishment was a successful journey through the [[Fire Rings of Fornax]],<ref name="app" /><ref name="negtd" /> an exceedingly difficult task<ref name="marvel">[[Star Wars (1977) 4|''Star Wars'' (1977) 4]]</ref> and one which saw Barkbone lose his sanity. Rufaan Tigellinus claimed to have witnessed the voyage. The Poss'Nomin freebooter lost one of his [[Limb/Legends|legs]] while plundering palaces on [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]], but he replaced the severed limb with a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] peg and returned to his piratical vocation.<ref name="app" /> On another occasion, the ''Robber Baron'' was pursued for a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] by the [[Loronar navy]], but Barkbone was not apprehended. The pirate claimed it was his least enjoyable interlude in [[Realspace/Legends|space]], because the sole [[COO-series cook droid/Legends|COO-series cook droid]] in the galley was programmed to produce only [[Vegetarian/Legends|vegetarian]] food.<ref name="negtd" />

===Imperial pursuit===
{{Quote|One way or another, I vow to wipe Reginald Barkbone from the fabric of space forever…|Grand Admiral Rufaan Tigellinus|<ref name="app" />}}
Barkbone clashed with members of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Guard]] on [[Axion]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] at some point. After pillaging the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], he attached a [[limpet mine]] to a [[GNK power droid/Legends|GNK power droid]] while running from pursuing Imperial Guards. The shell of a Gonk [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was extremely durable, but when cracked the internal generator exploded with force. Barkbone's [[Mine/Legends|mine]] achieved this, and he was able to shoot his way to freedom in the blast and resulting pandemonium.<ref name="negtd" /> He escaped, but the endeavor cost Barkbone one of his eyes and half his face.<ref name="app" />

The Imperial Navy stepped up its pursuit of Barkbone shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], and [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] Rufaan Tigellinus was tasked with his apprehension. In his [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], ''[[Avatar]]'', Tigellinus claimed to have doggedly chased the ''Robber Baron'' from the Colonies to the Outer Rim Territories. Around this time, Barkbone carried out a heist in the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]]; a [[Lightfoil/Legends|lightfoil]] was among his loot. Though Tigellinus's attempts to stop Barkbone's pilfering were fruitless, several of the Poss'Nomin's crew members were captured, and under interrogation they helped corroborate the Empire's records on Barkbone, separating fact from fiction. Tigellinus wrote a succinct report for [[Maximillian Seerdon]] of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] after a prolonged and futile pursuit. This report was in turn given to [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], placing Barkbone alongside other such pests to the Empire as [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] and [[Outlaw tech/Legends|outlaw tech]] [[Klaus Vandangante/Legends|Klaus Vandangante]]. Tigellinus was a rabid [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] and thus had personal motivation for the capture of Barkbone, a non-[[Human/Legends|Human]]. His report was disdainful of the Poss'Nomin's character and mental state, but Tigellinus recognized the threat Barkbone posed.<ref name="app" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|More cutthroat than the Thalassians, more underhanded than the Black Hole Gang, and more preposterous than Gir Kybo Ren-Cha…|Grand Admiral Rufaan Tigellinus|<ref name="app" />}}
Reginald Barkbone was a member of the physically imposing, three-eyed Poss'Nomin species.<ref name="app" /><ref name="ae">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> He was noted for his flamboyance<ref name="cswe" /> and his peculiar speech pattern, using a dialect of [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] described by Grand Admiral Tigellinus as "silly." Barkbone was also fluent in [[Lok Pirate's Cant]],<ref name="app" /> a private underworld parlance used by the pirates of [[Lok/Legends|Lok]].<ref name="gamer1">{{GamerCite|1|State of the Arts}}</ref> He owned a pet [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]], which was invariably perched on the pirate's shoulder, repeating every word he said. Barkbone took a fancy to the lightfoil, a weapon favored among the Tapani nobility, and began sporting a stolen lightfoil after a foray into Tapani territories. Tigellinus noted that his colleague, Grand Admiral [[Octavian Grant]], was infuriated that a brigand like the "Scourge of the Seven Sectors" was flaunting a symbol of Grant's own aristocracy.<ref name="app" /> Barkbone had a taste for meat, and was infuriated to be stuck with a vegetarian COO droid while on the run.<ref name="negtd" />

Barkbone was dedicated to his buccaneering lifestyle and continued his career even after a number of serious injuries. By the time of Tigellinus's chase for the Poss'Nomin, he had damaged his face and lost a leg. Barkbone subsequently procured a prosthetic replacement for his leg, making him a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]]. As attested by Tigellinus, Barkbone became insane in the course of his journey through the Fire Rings of [[Fornax/Legends|Fornax]].<ref name="app" />

Throughout his career, Barkbone proved to be an elusive target, avoiding capture on numerous occasions. The [[Loronar/Legends|Loronar]] navy pursued the Poss'Nomin for an entire month to no end,<ref name="negtd" /> and Tigellinus reportedly chased Barkbone from one end of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to another without success.<ref name="app" /> He was resourceful when attempting to escape from a tight situation. With Imperial Guards closing in on him, Barkbone attached explosives to a Gonk droid to impede his hunters.<ref name="negtd" /> According to Tigellinus, Barkbone was a bloodthirsty pirate, more vicious than even the notorious Thalassians, but he was also able to use trickery to beguile his adversaries. Throughout his life, Barkbone displayed a penchant for bold and audacious stunts, willingly navigating dangerous hazards.<ref name="app" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|p. 153: Here you'll see the first of two references to Reginald Barkbone, legendary pirate of the Seven Sectors. No tie-ins here. I just like pirates.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="wallaceblog">{{Blog|danwallace|54|Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 3 OF 3}}</ref>}}
Reginald Barkbone was created by [[Daniel Wallace]] for ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' in [[2006]]. Barkbone was presented with a "classic campy pirate feel,"<ref name="penablogcomments">{{Blog|abelgpena|70/comments|Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice and Wretched Rogues}}</ref> and was inserted because Wallace was partial to such pirates.<ref name="wallaceblog" /> While working on ''[[Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues]]'', the online supplement to [[Star Wars Insider 89|''Star Wars Insider'' 89]], [[Abel G. Peña]] realized no pirates had been included in the article; and having been amused by Barkbone's brief appearance in ''The New Essential Guide to Droids'', Peña decided to include him in the article. Wallace endorsed the expansion of Barkbone's backstory, with the caveat that he retained his character as an archetypal pirate. ''Underworld Appendix'', co-written by Peña and [[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]], was published several months after ''The New Essential Guide to Droids''.<ref name="penablogcomments" />
